science.title: Melanoma's secret to cheating death has finally been revealed
science.summary: Scientists have solved a long-standing mystery by discovering the missing genetic ingredient that helps melanoma cells become effectively immortal. The breakthrough could open the door to new treatments aimed at disrupting one of cancer's most important survival strategies.

science.title: A surprising brain discovery is forcing scientists to rethink movement disorders
science.summary: A surprising discovery is overturning a long-held assumption about how the brain’s movement center works. Researchers found that two key cerebellar cell types—thought to be tightly linked—often don’t behave in predictable ways, even though one directly influences the other. The finding suggests scientists may have been

science.title: Scientists discover a completely different way to fight viruses
science.summary: Researchers have uncovered an unexpected antiviral defense system in sea anemones that works very differently from the one humans use. The discovery suggests evolution developed multiple ways to combat viruses, challenging long-held ideas about how animal immune systems evolved.

science.title: Scientists discover a surprising link between vitamin C and brain health
science.summary: Could something as simple as vitamin C help support a healthier aging brain? In a study of more than 2,000 older adults in Japan, researchers found that people with lower vitamin C levels in their blood also tended to have less gray matter and weaker connections in a key brain network involved in memory, attention, and

science.title: After 70 years of excavation, ancient Sardis becomes a UNESCO World Heritage site
science.summary: After nearly seven decades of excavation, the legendary ancient city of Sardis has become a UNESCO World Heritage Site, celebrating years of discoveries that continue to reshape its history. Archaeologists say the biggest breakthroughs don't happen in a single season—they emerge as decades of evidence slowly come toget

tech.title: Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product
tech.summary: At an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers on Tuesday, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a major new product intended to support scientific research in the same way that Claude Code supports software engineering. Like Claude Code, Claude Science can autonomously carry out meaningful

tech.title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung
tech.summary: The news comes about a week after OpenAI announced its own custom AI chip in a partnership with Broadcom.

tech.title: Boeing-owned Wisk Aero accused of firing manager who raised safety concerns
tech.summary: A former software manager claims Wisk rushed software testing ahead of a crucial 2025 flight test.

tech.title: The Best Automatic Litter Box of 2026: Petkit and Litter-Robot
tech.summary: With these high-tech automatic litter boxes, gone are the days of scooping and smells. Welcome to the future.

tech.title: The Download: a startup has a solution for AI’s groupthink problem
tech.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give me a random number be

medicine.title: New drug unlocks elusive immune receptor, opening path toward motor neuron disease treatments
medicine.summary: University of Queensland researchers have developed a drug that activates a hard-to-target immune receptor—a breakthrough that could lead to new treatments for neurodegenerative conditions like motor neuron disease (MND).

medicine.title: 'Polypill' for heart failure cuts hospitalizations and ER visits by 60% in trial
medicine.summary: A "polypill" combining three medications recommended to treat heart failure into a single daily dose proved far more effective for patients than taking the drugs separately, a randomized clinical trial led by UT Southwestern Medical Center researchers showed. The findings, published in Nature Medicine, could lead to ne

medicine.title: Biomarker-matched drug combos shrink treatment-resistant melanoma in preclinical models
medicine.summary: A new study led by researchers at The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center has identified a way to tailor drug combinations based on specific tumor biology to improve outcomes for treatment-resistant advanced melanoma.

medicine.title: Two prostate cancer mutations reveal opposite responses to ferroptosis therapy
medicine.summary: A new study by researchers at The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center has identified genetic factors that determine whether prostate cancers are susceptible to a type of cell death known as ferroptosis. These findings, published in Nature Communications, could help guide treatment strategies for patients whos

medicine.title: Scientists may have finally found how Alzheimer's spreads through the brain
medicine.summary: A common brain protein may be giving Alzheimer’s disease an unexpected way to spread, carrying toxic Tau proteins from damaged neurons into healthy ones. By blocking these harmful protein packages before they reach new cells, researchers believe it may one day be possible to slow the disease's relentless progression.

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win 2026 Breakthrough, New Horizons prizes
mathematics.summary: Faculty member Shu-Heng Shao, in addition to four MIT alumni, are honored by the Breakthrough Prize Foundation.

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: Seven Perfect Shuffles Randomize a Deck of Cards. But How Many Sloppy Ones?
mathematics.summary: A decades-old proof showed that seven shuffles are enough to mix up a deck of cards. But it requires you to cut the deck with the precision of a professional magician. A new proof gets around that obstacle. The post Seven Perfect Shuffles Randomize a Deck of Cards. But How Many Sloppy Ones? first appeared on Quanta Mag

mathematics.title: How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math
mathematics.summary: With automated proof-checkers, a problem can be broken up into small chunks, solved bit-by-bit, then reassembled with confidence that every piece is correct. For some, this heralds a new area in mathematical research. The post How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up
mathematics.summary: By replacing the most fundamental concept in topology, Peter Scholze and Dustin Clausen are taking the first step in a far bigger program to understand why numbers behave the way they do. The post Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up first appeared on Quanta Magazine

space.title: Rocket issue delays NASA launch of rescue mission to save Swift space telescope
space.summary: Northrop Grumman has delayed the launch of its Pegasus XL rocket on a mission to save NASA's SWIFT space telescope from falling out of orbit.

space.title: 'Flying saucer' arrives at NASA for Artemis 3 moon mission in time for World UFO Day | Space photo of the day for July 2, 2026
space.summary: Just in time for UFO Day.

space.title: 'Rocket's Red Glare': How NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission celebrated America's 250th birthday
space.summary: NASA joined in the semiquincentennial celebrations this year by painting "America 250" on the rocket that launched the Artemis 2 astronauts around the moon.

space.title: 'PROMISE' me the moon? NASA wants to send spare nuclear-powered Mars rover to the lunar surface
space.summary: NASA has announced new lunar landing contracts for its Artemis Moon Base program, as well as a potential new moon rover mission named PROMISE.

space.title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launches 24 Starlink satellites from California
space.summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket carrying 24 Starlink satellites launched from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California on Wednesday, July 1, 2026.

astronomy.title: July Podcast: Spotlight on Spica
astronomy.summary: This month’s episode highlights the bright star Spica, now prominent high in the southwest after evening twilight. It’s leading the parade of constellations, along with the brilliant planet Venus, that will grace the Northern Hemisphere’s summer skies. astronomy.title: Exploding Stars Sprinkled Ancient Earth With Radioactive Iron and Plutonium
astronomy.summary: Exploding stars have left their radioactive mark on our planet. Now scientists have found more evidence of a particularly violent event in Earth’s more ancient past. The post Exploding Stars Sprinkled Ancient Earth With Radioactive Iron and Plutonium appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Astronomers Discover Terzan 5's True Nature
astronomy.summary: Observations of a distant cluster of stars in our galaxy have resulted in a new class of objects that turn out to be galactic building blocks. Researchers used Hubble Space Telescope (HST) and the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) to study the stars that make up Terzan 5, long though to be a globular cluster. What they

astronomy.title: The "Shadow Blaster" Galaxy's Role in High-energy Cosmic Neutrinos
astronomy.summary: On September 22, 2021, the IceCube Neutrino Detector in Antarctica caught a blast of neutrinos as it passed through the solar system. These neutrinos were remarkably high-energy and came from a galaxy 11 billion light-years away. That's a period of the Universe's history known as "Cosmic Noon". It's when star formation

astronomy.title: 'Stellar death is not the end': James Webb Space Telescope glimpses the fate of the solar system in a weird exoplanet orbiting a dead star
astronomy.summary: "It's like using a time machine to peer into the distant future of our solar system."

architecture.title: Dreaming in the Ruins: How a Sleeping Ritual in Logroño Proposes a New Civic Architecture
architecture.summary: Cities are increasingly designed to mitigate risk, and by doing so, need to collect data on climate, infrastructure, biodiversity, and social fragmentation so that the language of resilience becomes a fixture of planning. architecture.summary: Recent events highlighted the many ways architecture responds to changing environmental, social, and cultural conditions. architecture.title: Primus Office Building / Sanjay Puri Architects
architecture.summary: The Primus is a nine-level, climate-responsive office building in Jaipur, India, composed of a series of capsule-shaped modules stacked vertically to create a breathable and environmentally conscious workspace. Located adjacent to Jaipur Airport, the project was subject to a strict height restriction of 30 metres. To m

architecture.title: Billard Leece Partnership unveils Sydney children's hospital with folded metal facade
architecture.summary: Australian architecture studio Billard Leece Partnership has created an aluminium-clad acute-care facility for The Children's Hospital Westmead in Sydney, drawing on its inland river setting. architecture.title: Eight imaginative buildings with curving brickwork
architecture.summary: From a sculptural reading space to a turreted mausoleum, our latest roundup gathers curvy brick buildings where skillfully laid brickwork is shaped into fluid forms. construction.title: Letter to the Editor: There's Nothing New Under the Sun for Bridges
construction.summary: I get a bit amused by articles on “new” construction methods for bridges, such as the following one in Ohio.

construction.title: Correction: Wrong Casino Collapse Photo
construction.summary: Related Links: Engineering News Record Architectural Record ﻿The story "OSHA Points to Process Errors in Cincinnati Casino Collapse" contained errors. construction.title: BCIA shortlist revealed
construction.summary: The shortlist for this year’s British Construction & Infrastructure Awards (BCIAs) has been revealed. The post BCIA shortlist revealed appeared first on New Civil Engineer.

construction.title: Arcadis delivers net zero carbon station at Cambridge South
construction.summary: Design, engineering and consultancy firm Arcadis has announced the completion of Cambridge South train station, what it calls a “landmark net zero carbon railway station”. The post Arcadis delivers net zero carbon station at Cambridge South appeared first on New Civil Engineer.

construction.title: Private construction spending slid in May
construction.summary: Outside of the data center boom, spending on private projects weakened again, especially in the warehouse and office markets, according to ABC analysis of U.S. Census Bureau data.

ai.summary: arXiv:2607.00269v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLMs, solvers, and agent teams increasingly generate workflow actions, repairs, and plans, but a generated action may be syntactically valid yet stale, infeasible, conflicting, or destructive of the evidence that triggered a repair.
